texto: You know, usually with movies there are periods, dark areas, where I might not be getting what I wanted out of a theme. I'll have to go over and over it again.

texto: I grew up in such a musical family, and my dad was the first chair in the Johannesburg Symphony Orchestra, and my mom was a piano teacher and a painter, so it was kind of a creative environment, and it was kind of in my DNA.
explicacao: Citação de Trevor Rabin sobre temas ligados a familia. “I grew up in such a musical family, and my dad was the first chair in the Johannesburg Symphony O…” articula familia, family, mom em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como mom e creative.

texto: I belong in America more than South Africa. I can't remember the feeling of living there anymore. It's like it was in another life. That's sad in a way. It is my country. It's where I grew up. You don't know what it's like to have these negative feelings about your homeland. There are roots you can't escape.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Trevor Rabin: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I belong in America more than South Africa. I can't remember the feeling of living there anymore.…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, america, roots pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de america e roots.
